global_risks.description: Trump administration IEEPA tariffs on China, Canada, Mexico, EU. Average household tax increase $1,000 (2025) to $1,300 (2026).
global_risks.mitigation: Geographic diversification, domestic-focused companies, reduce China exposure
global_risks.original_text: President Trump has imposed International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) tariffs on US trading partners, including China, Canada, Mexico, and the EU... The Trump tariffs amount to an average tax increase per US household of $1,000 in 2025 and $1,300 in 2026.

global_risks.description: China military exercises near Taiwan in Dec 2025. CFR/ISW assess Taiwan as 'most dangerous flashpoint'. 2026 US NDS emphasizes Indo-Pacific.
global_risks.mitigation: Limit direct semiconductor exposure (TSMC), alternative supply chain beneficiaries
global_risks.original_text: China's live-fire drills around Taiwan at the end of December 2025 pushed the scope of its military exercises closer toward Taiwan than before—and showed that it could do so without warning at any time.

global_risks.description: June 2025 12-day war between Israel and Iran. US strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities. Iran enrichment up to 90%. Strait of Hormuz tanker seizures.
global_risks.mitigation: Energy sector hedging, monitor oil supply disruption
global_risks.original_text: On 13 June 2025, Israel launched a series of attacks against Iran's nuclear programme, its nuclear scientists, and senior commanders in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps... On 21-22 June 2025, the US launched military strikes against Iranian nuclear facilities in Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan.

global_risks.description: Trump administration ceasefire negotiations ongoing. Polymarket 45% chance of ceasefire by end 2026. Ukraine reconstruction 12-41 years depending on scenario.
global_risks.mitigation: Monitor European energy/defense opportunities, selective reconstruction plays
global_risks.original_text: Trump administration has initiated talks with Russia on a broad range of security issues, including a potential ceasefire agreement.

global_risks.description: Institutional consensus: low recession probability. US GDP growth 2.3% (2026). Consumer spending robust, AI capex continuing. Tariff effects and policy uncertainty are downside risks.
global_risks.mitigation: Focus on structural growth over cyclical plays, monitor leading indicators
global_risks.original_text: We look for U.S. real GDP growth of 2.3% (annual average) in 2026. The improved outlook reflects a more supportive fiscal policy environment, a less restrictive monetary policy setting.

global_risks.description: Core CPI down to 2.6% YoY (Dec 2025). Tariff-induced goods price pressure. Inflation likely to remain above 2% through 2026.
global_risks.mitigation: Companies with pricing power, real assets exposure, TIPS consideration
global_risks.original_text: Core CPI was 2.6% year-over-year in December, down from 3.1% in August. The direction of travel for core inflation entering 2026 is clear... We expect inflation to still be above 2% by the end of next year.

global_risks.description: Global growth 3.3% (below historical 3.7% average). China property crisis ongoing, export weakness from trade war.
global_risks.mitigation: Reduce direct China exposure, focus on supply chain relocation beneficiaries
global_risks.original_text: Global growth is projected at 3.3 percent both in 2025 and 2026, below the historical (2000-19) average of 3.7 percent... downward revisions in other major economies.

global_risks.description: Debate ongoing: GMO warns of classic bubble, Goldman Sachs sees fundamental support. Key difference from dot-com: profitable companies with strong balance sheets.
global_risks.mitigation: Avoid AI overconcentration, diversify to secondary beneficiaries, balance with non-AI growth
global_risks.original_text: AI looks like a classic investment bubble to us, with very high valuations and signs of rampant speculation. But we recognize that while many investors harbor fears that AI might be a bubble, they are far from sure of that fact.

global_risks.description: CUSMA review uncertainty, tariff policy volatility, regulatory changes (semiconductors, big tech, energy).
global_risks.mitigation: Balance policy beneficiaries/losers, US onshoring plays, flexible tactical rebalancing
global_risks.original_text: With geopolitical uncertainty elevated and Canada's trade agreement with the United States and Mexico under review, risks around the outlook are unusually high.

bull_case.title: Bull Scenario (Optimistic)
bull_case.trigger: Trade war de-escalation, Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, Fed rate cuts to 3%, AI productivity proven, geopolitical tensions eased

asset_allocation.focus: Growth stocks (AI, semiconductors, biotech), increase EM exposure

base_case.title: Base Scenario (Current Trajectory)
base_case.trigger: Trade war stalemate at 2025 levels, manageable geopolitical tensions, Fed at 3.00-3.25%, US GDP 2.0-2.5%
key_assumptions: Trade war fatigue limits further escalation

asset_allocation.focus: Balance growth and value, maintain 30% safe assets (regulatory compliance)

bear_case.title: Bear Scenario (Pessimistic)
bear_case.trigger: Taiwan Strait military conflict, Iran nuclear escalation, extreme tariff escalation (60%+ on all China), inflation reacceleration forcing Fed hikes, AI bubble burst

asset_allocation.strategy: For 31-year horizon, bear scenario is buying opportunity
asset_allocation.action: No panic selling, execute staged buying plan at -10%, -20%, -30% levels
